audit_result.proof_trace: `auctionCallback` decodes user-supplied `solution` bytes into a `Call[]` array and passes it to `_multiCall` (Caller.sol L58-60) with no inspection of call targets or encoded selectors. `_multiCall` issues raw low-level `.call(data)` per entry, so any ERC20 `approve(arbitrarySpender, amount)` payload in the solution executes unconditionally; the only post-`_multiCall` approval at L111 covers only `assetOut`→auction for `amountOut` and does not undo rogue allowances on other tokens.
audit_result.attack_scenario: A holder of AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE calls `bid()` with a `solution` containing `Call{target: LINK_token, data: approve(attackerEOA, type(uint256).max)}`. This is decoded in `auctionCallback` and executed by `_multiCall`, granting the attacker an unlimited spending allowance on the AuctionBidder contract's LINK balance, which the attacker then drains via `transferFrom`.

PROP-01b-partial2-inv-004.text: Solution _multiCall must not approve contract tokens to addresses other than the auction contract.
PROP-01b-partial2-inv-004.assertion: forall _call in auctionCallback._multiCall: if target is ERC20 and data encodes approve(spender, amt) then spender == s_auction
